machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane secretin ring (export channel through the OM)
machinery_genes.quote: grafting fluorescent proteins onto several components, YscC (outer-membrane (OM) ring), YscD (forms the inner-membrane (IM) ring together with YscJ), YscN (ATPase), and YscQ (putative C ring)
machinery_genes.note: Named as the OM ring of the Yersinia Ysc injectisome. Originally identified in the virC operon of Y. enterocolitica as a PulD-family secretin (Michiels 1991, PMID 1860816: 'Protein YscC has a putative signal sequence and shares significant homology with outer membrane proteins involved in the secretion of pullulanase by Klebsiella pneumoniae (PulD)'). Ysc apparatus is the Y. pestis/enterocolitica/pseudotuberculosis pYV-encoded archetype; gene set is shared across the genus.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane MS-ring (connector linking OM secretin to IM ring)
machinery_genes.quote: YscD (forms the inner-membrane (IM) ring together with YscJ)
machinery_genes.note: IM ring component; Diepold 2010 places it as the connector between YscC and YscJ. Michiels 1991 (PMID 1860816) already showed yscD required for Yop export.

machinery_genes.quote: YscD (forms the inner-membrane (IM) ring together with YscJ)
machinery_genes.note: IM ring lipoprotein. Michiels 1991 (PMID 1860816): 'YscJ turned out to be YlpB, a lipoprotein' and yscJ required for Yop export.

machinery_genes.quote: The assembly of the export apparatus (YscR,S,T,U,V) of the Yersinia type III secretion apparatus occurs independently of other structural components
machinery_genes.note: Member of the IM export apparatus (YscR,S,T,U,V) named in the Diepold 2011 title and abstract for the Yersinia Ysc system.

machinery_genes.quote: Oligomerization required the cytosolic domain of YscV, as well as YscR, -S, -T, but no other Ysc protein
machinery_genes.note: Export apparatus component; named alongside YscR and YscT as required for YscV oligomerization in the Yersinia Ysc IM platform.

machinery_genes.quote: Oligomerization required the cytosolic domain of YscV, as well as YscR, -S, -T, but no other Ysc protein
machinery_genes.note: Export apparatus component of the Yersinia Ysc injectisome.

machinery_genes.quote: the other export apparatus components YscR, YscS, YscT, and YscU
machinery_genes.note: Named explicitly as an export apparatus component of the Yersinia T3SS (SctU/FlhB family autoprotease switch).

machinery_genes.quote: YscV (FlhA in the flagellum) is an essential component of the inner membrane (IM) export apparatus of the type III secretion injectisome.
machinery_genes.note: Central export-apparatus ring (SctV/FlhA family); equivalent to LcrD in earlier Yersinia literature.

machinery_genes.quote: We conclude that YscN is a component of the Yop secretion machinery using ATP. We hypothesize that it is either the energizer of this machinery
machinery_genes.note: ATPase of the Yersinia Yop secretion machinery (Woestyn 1994); also named 'YscN (ATPase)' in Diepold 2010 (PMID 20453832).

machinery_genes.quote: YscN and YscQ attached synchronously, requiring each other, the interacting proteins YscK and YscL, but no further injectisome component for their assembly
machinery_genes.note: Named as part of the cytosolic complex (sorting platform / ATPase stator). Michiels 1991 (PMID 1860816) showed yscL required for Yop export.

machinery_genes.quote: YscN and YscQ attached synchronously, requiring each other, the interacting proteins YscK and YscL, but no further injectisome component for their assembly
machinery_genes.note: Cytosolic sorting-platform component (SctK family) of the Yersinia Ysc injectisome.

machinery_genes.quote: YscC (outer-membrane (OM) ring), YscD ... YscN (ATPase), and YscQ (putative C ring)
machinery_genes.note: C-ring / sorting platform (SctQ/SpaO family) of the Yersinia Ysc injectisome.

machinery_genes.quote: the TTSS needle complex protein, YscF, was selected to investigate its potential as a protective antigen
machinery_genes.note: Needle subunit named directly in Yersinia pestis (the organism of this instance). Matson 2005 characterizes YscF as 'a surface-expressed protein of the Yersinia pestis type III secretion complex'.

machinery_genes.role: molecular ruler that sets needle length (substrate-specificity switch)
machinery_genes.quote: we provide evidence that the length of this needle is determined by the size of a protein, YscP, acting as a molecular ruler
machinery_genes.note: Needle-length ruler (Journet 2003). Confirmed by Agrain 2005 (PMID 16102009): 'The length of the needle of the Yersinia Ysc injectisome is determined by a protein called YscP.'

machinery_genes.quote: The outer membrane YscC secretin is a major part of Yersinia enterocolitica's injectisome and is among the first components to assemble, solely assisted by its pilotin, YscW.
machinery_genes.note: Pilotin (VirG) that pilots secretin YscC to the OM (Kowal 2013). Accessory but a bona fide apparatus assembly factor, not a regulator or effector. Functional pilotin role independently confirmed by the yscW homolog ExsB in Pseudomonas (PMID 25690097).

machinery_genes.quote: Three of the Yops, namely YopB, YopD and LcrV, are required for the translocation of the others across the target cell membrane. They form some kind of a pore in the target cell membrane.
machinery_genes.note: Translocon pore component. Per the apparatus brief, translocators (host-membrane pore) count as machinery, not effectors, despite being secreted.

machinery_genes.quote: Three of the Yops, namely YopB, YopD and LcrV, are required for the translocation of the others across the target cell membrane. They form some kind of a pore in the target cell membrane.
machinery_genes.note: Translocon pore component (with YopB and LcrV); apparatus per brief, not an effector.

machinery_genes.role: needle-tip / translocator that assembles the host-membrane pore
machinery_genes.quote: Three of the Yops, namely YopB, YopD and LcrV, are required for the translocation of the others across the target cell membrane. They form some kind of a pore in the target cell membrane.
machinery_genes.note: Tip protein / translocator (V antigen) required for pore formation. Counted as apparatus (tip/translocon) per the brief. Note it also has a regulatory role, but its tip-complex/translocation function is structural.

notes: System: the pYV-plasmid-encoded Ysc-Yop type III secretion system (injectisome) of pathogenic Yersinia, the archetypal Ysc/Sct injectisome that injects Yop effectors into host cells. In Y. pestis CO92 the ysc/lcr/vir genes and yop effectors sit on plasmid pCD1 (NC_003131.1), the effector context for this instance. The Ysc apparatus is functionally identical across Y. pestis, Y. enterocolitica and Y. pseudotuberculosis (same ysc gene set on the homologous virulence plasmid); most mechanistic apparatus papers used Y. enterocolitica (Michiels 1991, Woestyn 1994, Journet 2003, Agrain 2005, Diepold 2010/2011, Kowal 2013), with the needle subunit YscF named directly in Y. pestis (Matson 2005). Strain transfer noted per gene. Core apparatus covered gene-by-gene: OM secretin YscC + pilotin YscW; IM rings YscD, YscJ; export apparatus YscR, YscS, YscT, YscU, YscV; ATPase YscN with stator/sorting-platform YscK, YscL, YscQ; needle YscF and its length ruler YscP; translocon YopB, YopD, LcrV. Translocators (YopB/YopD/LcrV) included as apparatus per the T3SS brief (host-membrane pore), not as effectors. Deliberately EXCLUDED: secreted effectors of this instance (YopE/H/M/T, YpkA, etc.); the secretion chaperones/Syc proteins (e.g. SycD/LcrH, SycE) that escort translocators/effectors; transcriptional regulators (VirF/LcrF, the LcrG/LcrV-linked regulatory functions); and the gatekeeper YopN/TyeA (SctW) which is a secretion-control 'plug' rather than a structural channel component (omitted to stay conservative; could be added as SctW if the benchmark wants the gatekeeper class). YscO (SctO stalk) and YscI (SctI inner rod) exist in the operon (named in Michiels 1991 as yscA-yscM) but were not given an individual structural-naming quote here, so they were omitted under quality-over-completeness; their omission does not affect the COMPLETE core call. Lowest-confidence call: LcrV, which is dual-function (tip/translocon structure + regulation); listed for its structural tip-complex role.
